events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes DP impulse lines sloped wrong for service - gas taps below pipe, liquid taps on top. This is a classic installation error. The work is in-service. This is non-compliant and will cause measurement errors. The transmitter is installed defective/non-compliant. Since it's in service with wrong impulse line routing, it will give erroneous readings - for gas service, taps below pipe will collect liquid; for liquid service, taps on top will collect gas. This degrades measurement accuracy and can lead to failed readings.

Decision: fail (non-compliant, dangerous)
Risk: high
Work stage: in-service
Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant, degraded (measurement degraded)
Percent complete: 100 (it's in service)
Confidence: high

    "Incorrect impulse line slope/orientation will trap liquid in gas service or gas in liquid service, causing erroneous DP measurement",
    "Installation non-compliant with API RP 551 and manufacturer installation requirements"

  "actions": [
    "Isolate and depressurize transmitter impulse lines per permit",
    "Reroute/repipe impulse lines with correct slope: gas service taps above pipe sloping down to transmitter; liquid service taps below pipe sloping up to transmitter",
    "Verify slope (typically 1:12 or per spec) with no sags or traps",
    "Recommission and verify calibration/span after correction"
  ],
  "rationale": "The impulse lines are installed with the wrong orientation for the process service. For gas service, taps must be above the pipe so condensate drains back; for liquid service, taps must be below so gas vents back. The described condition (gas taps below, liquid taps on top) is the classic reversed arrangement that traps the wrong phase, degrading or failing DP measurement. Since the asset is in service, erroneous measurement can cause improper process control, making this high risk and non-compliant.",
  "references": ["API RP 551", "Manufacturer installation manuals"]
}
